mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and real economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals imminent recession as labor market slack reduces aggregate demand and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) and decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand without labor market overheating, supporting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or directional accuracy is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, rising consumer price inflation signals persistent monetary tightening and potential Fed overshoot, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Within the regime, rising CPI fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the rank IC is below 0.04 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based demand strength that lifts corporate revenues and expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal's rank IC is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns follow in less than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points signals an unexpected easing of monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by at least 25bp in a month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases following a 25bp+ cut, or the average excess return over the baseline is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal a healthy labor market with rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 2-year rolling windows is below 0.03, or 5-day returns are positive in less than 55% of instances when PAYEMS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real GDP and industrial production signal a balanced expansion of both demand and supply, reinforcing corporate profit growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of instances when both series are rising.
